title: Mastering Microservices
subtitle: From Foundation to Distributed Scale
author: Wordsus Team
description: A comprehensive guide to building, deploying, and scaling microservices. Learn how to design service boundaries, manage decentralized data, implement event-driven architectures, and ensure resilience and security in complex distributed systems.

chapters.title: The Evolution of Software Architecture
chapters.description: Trace the journey from monolithic systems to modern microservices, understanding the trade-offs and the role of cloud computing.

chapters.title: Core Concepts and Principles
chapters.description: Master the fundamental principles of microservices: independent deployability, loose coupling, high cohesion, and decentralized governance.

chapters.title: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Primer
chapters.description: Learn the essentials of DDD, including ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, and context mapping, to model services around business domains.

chapters.title: Organizational Impact and Culture
chapters.description: Explore Conway's Law, cross-functional teams, and the DevOps culture required to successfully build and run microservices.

chapters.title: Defining Service Boundaries
chapters.description: Identify business capabilities, use Event Storming, and apply patterns like the Strangler Fig to define effective service boundaries.

chapters.title: API Design and Contracts
chapters.description: Master REST, gRPC, and GraphQL principles, and learn about consumer-driven contract testing and versioning strategies.

chapters.title: Data Management in Decentralized Systems
chapters.description: Handle data in microservices using the database-per-service pattern, Sagas, CQRS, and event sourcing.

chapters.title: Event-Driven Architecture Fundamentals
chapters.description: Understand events, commands, pub-sub models, and how to design idempotent consumers for robust event-driven systems.

chapters.title: Synchronous Communication Patterns
chapters.description: Implement effective synchronous communication with HTTP/REST, managing timeouts, retries, and circuit breakers.

chapters.title: Asynchronous Messaging and Message Brokers
chapters.description: Deep dive into message brokers, delivery guarantees, dead letter queues, and competing consumer patterns.

chapters.title: Service Discovery and Registry
chapters.description: Learn why dynamic service discovery is essential and how to implement client-side and server-side discovery algorithms.

chapters.title: API Gateways and Edge Services
chapters.description: Understand the role of API Gateways, request routing, API composition, and the Backend for Frontend (BFF) pattern.

chapters.title: Containerization Fundamentals
chapters.description: Master Linux containers, image creation, multi-stage builds, and container security best practices.

chapters.title: Orchestration and Cluster Management
chapters.description: Explore workload management, internal networking, and configuration decoupling in container orchestration platforms.

chapters.title: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD)
chapters.description: Build autonomous deployment pipelines and implement strategies like Blue-Green and Canary releases.

chapters.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
chapters.description: Manage cloud resources declaratively, implement immutable infrastructure, and handle configuration drift.

chapters.title: Designing for Failure (Resilience Engineering)
chapters.description: Embrace the fallacies of distributed computing, apply Chaos Engineering, and design for graceful degradation.

chapters.title: Security in Distributed Systems
chapters.description: Implement defense in depth, mTLS, zero trust concepts, and protect against common API vulnerabilities.

chapters.title: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
chapters.description: Decouple authentication and authorization using OAuth 2.0, OIDC, and JWTs across microservices.

chapters.title: Distributed Logging and Tracing
chapters.description: Implement centralized logging, correlation IDs, and distributed tracing using standards like OpenTelemetry.

chapters.title: Metrics, Monitoring, and Alerting
chapters.description: Monitor the four golden signals, handle metric cardinality, and create actionable alerting rules with SLOs and SLIs.

chapters.title: Testing Strategies for Microservices
chapters.description: Adapt the testing pyramid for microservices, using mocks, ephemeral environments, and smart integration tests.

chapters.title: Service Meshes
chapters.description: Understand when to use a service mesh, its architecture, sidecar proxies, and advanced traffic management.

chapters.title: Migrating from Monolith to Microservices
chapters.description: Assess readiness and apply migration strategies like Change Data Capture and the Strangler pattern.

chapters.title: Microservices Anti-Patterns and Pitfalls
chapters.description: Avoid common pitfalls like distributed monoliths, mega-services, and synchronous chains of death.

parts.title: Part VII: Advanced Topics and Anti-Patterns
